title: Pterospermum xylocarpum
intro: Pterospermum xylocarpum, also known as the sterculia tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sterculiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and skin disorders, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pterospermum xylocarpum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Pterospermum xylocarpum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Pterospermum, which comprises about 150 species. The Malvaceae family, also known as the mallow family, is a large and diverse group of plants that includes hibiscus, okra, and hollyhock. Pterospermum xylocarpum is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This plant is also known as the Indian staff tree, and its seeds are often used in traditional medicine.
